# services.anubis

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.anubis`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions` | `submodule` | Default options for all instances of Anubis. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable this instance of Anubis. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.extraFlags` | `list of string` | A list of extra flags to be passed to Anubis. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.group` | `string` | The group under which Anubis is run. This module utilizes systemd's DynamicUser feature. See the corresponding section in {manpage}`systemd.exec(5)` for more details. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.policy` | `submodule` | Anubis policy configuration. See [the documentation](https://anubis.techaro.lol/docs/admin/policies) for details. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.policy.extraBots` | `list of (JSON value)` | Additional bot rules appended to the policy. When {option}`useDefaultBotRules` is `true`, these rules are added after Anubis's default rules. When `false`, only these rules are used. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.policy.settings` | `JSON value` | Additional policy settings merged into the policy file. Common settings include `dnsbl`, `store`, `logging`, `thresholds`, `impressum`, `openGraph`, and `statusCodes`. See [the documentation](https://anubis.techaro.lol/docs/admin/policies) for available options. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.policy.useDefaultBotRules` | `boolean` | Whether to include Anubis's default bot detection rules via the `(data)/meta/default-config.yaml` import. Set to `false` to define your own bot rules from scratch using {option}`extraBots`. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (null or string or signed integer or boolean)` | Freeform configuration via environment variables for Anubis. See [the documentation](https://anubis.techaro.lol/docs/admin/installation) for a complete list of available environment variables. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.settings.BIND_NETWORK` | `string` | The network family that Anubis should bind to. Accepts anything supported by Go's [`net.Listen`](https://pkg.go.dev/net#Listen). Common values are `tcp` and `unix`. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.settings.DIFFICULTY` | `signed integer` | The difficulty required for clients to solve the challenge. Currently, this means the amount of leading zeros in a successful response. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.settings.METRICS_BIND_NETWORK` | `string` | The network family that the metrics server should bind to. Accepts anything supported by Go's [`net.Listen`](https://pkg.go.dev/net#Listen). Common values are `tcp` and `unix`. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.settings.OG_PASSTHROUGH` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Open Graph tag passthrough. This enables social previews of resources protected by Anubis without having to exempt each scraper individually. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.settings.POLICY_FNAME` | `null or absolute path` | The policy file to use. Leave this as `null` to use the policy generated from {option}`services.anubis.instances.<name>.policy`. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.settings.SERVE_ROBOTS_TXT` | `boolean` | Whether to serve a default robots.txt that denies access to common AI bots by name and all other bots by wildcard. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.settings.WEBMASTER_EMAIL` | `null or string` | If set, shows a contact email address when rendering error pages. This email address will be how users can get in contact with administrators. |
| `services.anubis.defaultOptions.user` | `string` | The user under which Anubis is run. This module utilizes systemd's DynamicUser feature. See the corresponding section in {manpage}`systemd.exec(5)` for more details. |
| `services.anubis.instances` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | An attribute set of Anubis instances. The attribute name may be an empty string, in which case the `-<name>` suffix is not added to the service name and socket paths. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable this instance of Anubis. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.extraFlags` | `list of string` | A list of extra flags to be passed to Anubis. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.group` | `string` | The group under which Anubis is run. This module utilizes systemd's DynamicUser feature. See the corresponding section in {manpage}`systemd.exec(5)` for more details. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.policy` | `submodule` | Anubis policy configuration. See [the documentation](https://anubis.techaro.lol/docs/admin/policies) for details. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.policy.extraBots` | `list of (JSON value)` | Additional bot rules appended to the policy. When {option}`useDefaultBotRules` is `true`, these rules are added after Anubis's default rules. When `false`, only these rules are used. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.policy.settings` | `JSON value` | Additional policy settings merged into the policy file. Common settings include `dnsbl`, `store`, `logging`, `thresholds`, `impressum`, `openGraph`, and `statusCodes`. See [the documentation](https://anubis.techaro.lol/docs/admin/policies) for available options. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.policy.useDefaultBotRules` | `boolean` | Whether to include Anubis's default bot detection rules via the `(data)/meta/default-config.yaml` import. Set to `false` to define your own bot rules from scratch using {option}`extraBots`. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings` | `open submodule of attribute set of (null or string or signed integer or boolean)` | Freeform configuration via environment variables for Anubis. See [the documentation](https://anubis.techaro.lol/docs/admin/installation) for a complete list of available environment variables. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.BIND` | `string` | The address that Anubis listens to. See Go's [`net.Listen`](https://pkg.go.dev/net#Listen) for syntax. When using unix sockets: - use the prefix "/run/anubis/anubis/" if the instance name is the empty string, - "/run/anubis/anubis-<name>/" otherwise. Defaults to Unix domain sockets. To use TCP sockets, set this to a TCP address and `BIND_NETWORK` to `"tcp"`. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.BIND_NETWORK` | `string` | The network family that Anubis should bind to. Accepts anything supported by Go's [`net.Listen`](https://pkg.go.dev/net#Listen). Common values are `tcp` and `unix`. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.DIFFICULTY` | `signed integer` | The difficulty required for clients to solve the challenge. Currently, this means the amount of leading zeros in a successful response. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.METRICS_BIND` | `string` | The address Anubis' metrics server listens to. See Go's [`net.Listen`](https://pkg.go.dev/net#Listen) for syntax. When using unix sockets: - use the prefix "/run/anubis/anubis/" if the instance name is the empty string, - "/run/anubis/anubis-<name>/" otherwise. The metrics server is enabled by default and may be disabled. However, due to implementation details, this is only possible by setting a command line flag. See {option}`services.anubis.defaultOptions.extraFlags` for an example. Defaults to Unix domain sockets. To use TCP sockets, set this to a TCP address and `METRICS_BIND_NETWORK` to `"tcp"`. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.METRICS_BIND_NETWORK` | `string` | The network family that the metrics server should bind to. Accepts anything supported by Go's [`net.Listen`](https://pkg.go.dev/net#Listen). Common values are `tcp` and `unix`. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.OG_PASSTHROUGH` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Open Graph tag passthrough. This enables social previews of resources protected by Anubis without having to exempt each scraper individually. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.POLICY_FNAME` | `null or absolute path` | The policy file to use. Leave this as `null` to use the policy generated from {option}`services.anubis.instances.<name>.policy`. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.SERVE_ROBOTS_TXT` | `boolean` | Whether to serve a default robots.txt that denies access to common AI bots by name and all other bots by wildcard. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.TARGET` | `string` | The reverse proxy target that Anubis is protecting. This is a required option. The usage of Unix domain sockets is supported by the following syntax: `unix:///path/to/socket.sock`. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.settings.WEBMASTER_EMAIL` | `null or string` | If set, shows a contact email address when rendering error pages. This email address will be how users can get in contact with administrators. |
| `services.anubis.instances.<name>.user` | `string` | The user under which Anubis is run. This module utilizes systemd's DynamicUser feature. See the corresponding section in {manpage}`systemd.exec(5)` for more details. |
| `services.anubis.package` | `package` | The anubis package to use. |
